title: goto main_loop pattern with variable declarations
description:
main.c:98-102 declares variables before the goto targets to avoid jumping over declarations. The pattern works but the code flow is unusual: variables declared, then goto potentially skips their use, then main_loop label.

What goes wrong as-is: Reader must understand C's rules about jumping over declarations. The comment helps but the structure is awkward.

Alternative: Extract the standalone LoRa init block into a separate function that returns success/failure, then call it from main(). This eliminates the goto and makes the flow linear. The variables would be local to the helper function.
close_reason: Won't fix: goto pattern is C-correct and commented, refactor adds complexity
